title: Alien: Romulus Joins Prometheus As Only Second Alien Movie To Reach Impressive Box Office Milestone
text: The 2024 science fiction horror film Alien: Romulus has achieved a significant commercial milestone by crossing the $300 million mark in global box office revenue. According to tracking data, the film is projected to earn approximately $314.4 million worldwide by the end of its fourth weekend in theaters. This accomplishment makes Alien: Romulus only the second installment in the franchise to surpass the $300 million threshold, following Prometheus from 2012. The article notes that "it is only the tenth 2024 release to pass this milestone, with the most recent beforehand being the record-smashing Marvel Cinematic Universe hit "Deadpool & Wolverine"." Directed by Fede Álvarez and starring Cailee Spaeny, the film takes place chronologically between the original 1979 Alien and its 1986 sequel Aliens. Despite this achievement, analysis suggests the movie will likely remain second to Prometheus, which earned $403.3 million globally. The article observes that Romulus "has trailed consistently behind the 2012 title" throughout its theatrical run, making it unlikely to close the $88.9 million gap needed to claim the top position.
